assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1.

The module utilizes a 4-bit counter (cnt) to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received. Every eight din_serial input, dout_parallel will output. When all 8 serial data bits have been received, the valid output signal is set to 1, indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0, indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: All added cases use only ports declared in the contract.
LLM planning note: Timing assumptions remain conservative due to the unknown latency model.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
A single 8‑bit valid sequence results in dout_parallel reflecting the transmitted bits (MSB first) and dout_valid asserting high after the last bit.
If din_valid is deasserted before eight bits are collected, dout_valid remains low.
cases.goal: Confirm correct accumulation of serial bits into the parallel word and proper valid signaling under nominal conditions.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Stimulus respects the ambiguous ready semantics by keeping din_valid high throughout the sequence.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['clk', 'rst_n', 'din_serial', 'din_valid']
Drive a known 8‑bit pattern (e.g., 8'b1011_0110) on din_serial with din_valid asserted for each bit, synchronized to clk.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe dout_parallel and dout_valid after at least one rising edge following the eighth valid input; no exact-cycle latency is assumed.

expected_properties: When din_valid is deasserted, changes on din_serial must not affect dout_parallel or dout_valid.
cases.goal: Verify that the design ignores serial data when din_valid is low, preserving output stability.
notes: Ensures that the valid handshake semantics are respected despite the lack of an explicit ready signal.
preconditions: Design has been reset and is in a known idle state.
stimulus_intent: Toggle din_serial with din_valid low for several cycles, then assert din_valid and provide a valid 8‑bit sequence.
timing_assumptions: Observe that dout_parallel and dout_valid remain unchanged during the din_valid low period.
After din_valid is asserted, expect dout_valid to become high after the eighth valid bit.

expected_properties: The module should not assert dout_valid until exactly eight din_valid‑qualified bits have been received.
dout_valid must be deasserted when a new sequence starts.
cases.goal: Exercise the implicit valid‑only protocol and verify correct assertion/deassertion of dout_valid.
notes: Addresses the contract ambiguity regarding missing ready signals.
preconditions: Module is out of reset and dout_valid is low.
stimulus_intent: Provide a continuous stream of din_serial bits with din_valid high, then insert a gap (din_valid low) before starting a new 8‑bit word.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume a fixed latency; monitor dout_valid for the first time it becomes high after each 8‑bit group.
